Key_Symptoms: Bone pain, anemia, renal failure, hypercalcemia (CRAB criteria)
Clinical_Notes: Most common primary bone marrow cancer; incidence rising globally

Key_Symptoms: Fatigue, bruising, infections, bleeding, bone pain
Standard_Treatment: 7+3 induction (Cytarabine + Daunorubicin); Allogeneic SCT; Targeted therapy
Clinical_Notes: Most common acute leukemia in adults; highly aggressive

Key_Symptoms: Fatigue, bone pain, lymphadenopathy, CNS symptoms
Standard_Treatment: Multi-agent chemotherapy; CNS prophylaxis; Allogeneic SCT; CAR-T
Clinical_Notes: Most common childhood cancer; 90%+ cure rate in children

Key_Symptoms: Often asymptomatic; fatigue, splenomegaly, night sweats
Standard_Treatment: Tyrosine kinase inhibitors (Imatinib, Dasatinib, Nilotinib); Allogeneic SCT
Clinical_Notes: Paradigm of targeted therapy; near-normal life expectancy with TKIs

Key_Genetic_Markers: del(13q), del(11q), del(17p), trisomy 12, TP53 mutation
Clinical_Notes: Most common leukemia in Western countries; often indolent course

Key_Genetic_Markers: del(5q), SF3B1, TET2, ASXL1, DNMT3A, TP53
Standard_Treatment: Azacitidine, Decitabine; Lenalidomide (del5q); Luspatercept; Allogeneic SCT
Clinical_Notes: Pre-leukemic condition; 30% progress to AML; increasing in elderly
Chronic MPN; risk of myelofibrosis and AML transformation
Generally favorable prognosis; near-normal life expectancy

Key_Genetic_Markers: JAK2 V617F (55%), CALR (30%), MPL (8%); ASXL1, SRSF2
Key_Symptoms: Anemia, massive splenomegaly, constitutional symptoms, bone marrow fibrosis
Standard_Treatment: Ruxolitinib; Fedratinib; Allogeneic SCT; Supportive care
Clinical_Notes: Most aggressive MPN; only cure is allogeneic SCT
Rare indolent lymphoma with bone marrow involvement

Key_Symptoms: Heart failure, nephrotic syndrome, neuropathy, macroglossia
Standard_Treatment: Daratumumab + CyBorD; Autologous SCT; Organ support
Clinical_Notes: Misfolded light chains deposit in organs; early diagnosis critical
Excellent prognosis; single course of cladribine often curative
